response: 439 is a prime number. It is not divisible by any prime numbers less than its square root (≈20.95), including 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, and 19.
reasoning_content:
We need to determine if 439 is a prime number. A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself. So we need to check if 439 has any divisors besides 1 and itself.

First, note that 439 is odd, so not divisible by 2. Check divisibility by 3: sum of digits 4+3+9=16, not divisible by 3, so no. Divisible by 5? Last digit not 0 or 5, so no. Next, check 7: 7*62=434, remainder 5, so not divisible. 11: 11*39=429, remainder 10, so no. 13: 13*33=429, remainder 10; 13*34=442, too high, so no. 17: 17*25=425, remainder 14; 17*26=442, too high. 19: 19*23=437, remainder 2; 19*24=456, too high. 23: 23*19=437, remainder 2; 23*20=460. 29: 29*15=435, remainder 4; 29*16=464. 31: 31*14=434, remainder 5; 31*15=465. 37: 37*11=407, remainder 32; 37*12=444, too high. 41: 41*10=410, remainder 29; 41*11=451. 43: 43*10=430, remainder 9; 43*11=473. We should check up to sqrt(439). sqrt(439) ≈ 20.95, because 20^2=400, 21^2=441. So we only need to check primes up to 20. That list: 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. We've checked all those, none divide evenly. So 439 is prime. Sanity check: 439 mod 7 = 5, mod 11 = 10, mod 13 = 10, mod 17 = 14, mod 19 = 2. So yes, prime.
